docs.title: My Digital Garden
docs.text:
Welcome! I am Abby, an undergraduate student in the Turing Class (Computer Science and Technology) at Zhejiang University.

This website is a live record of my intellectual growth and technical journey. It serves as a centralized repository where I document my ongoing explorations into the frontiers of research.

docs.title: 📚 What I am doing here
docs.text:
• Mathematical Foundations: Building a solid base in Analysis, Algebra, and Probability.
  • Programming & Systems: Mastering languages (C/C++, Python) and hardware architecture (Verilog).
  • Research Exploration: Investigating Computer Vision and Embodied AI and so on.
  • Toolkit & Workflows: Curating efficient software practices and site maintenance guides.

docs.title: 🚀 Continuous Learning
docs.text:
I believe in "Learning in Public." This site is under constant construction as I acquire new skills and refine my understanding. Every update here represents a step forward in my academic and professional path.

Stay curious. Keep building.

Last updated: 2026 | Built with MkDocs

docs.title: Mathematics

docs.text:

  • Desmos Graphing Calculator - Best for visualizing functions.
  • Overleaf (LaTeX) - For professional academic writing.
\[ f(x) = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{f^{(n)}(a)}{n!} (x-a)^n \] \[ \hat{f}(\xi) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(x) e^{-2\pi i x \xi} dx \]

“Mathematics is the language in which God has written the universe.”

docs.title: Mathematical Analysis: The Calculus of Rigor
docs.text:
——where intuition meets \(\epsilon - \delta\) 🔍

Mathematical Analysis is the formalization of "closeness." For a CS student interested in 3D Vision and Embodied AI, this isn't just about solving integrals—it's about understanding the continuity and optimization of the physical world.

docs.title: 📝 To-Do List (Spring 2026)
docs.text:
• Multivariable Calculus: Partial derivatives, Gradients, and the Jacobian matrix.
  • Sequences & Series: Understanding convergence and Taylor approximations.
  • Optimization Foundations: Lagrange multipliers and Hessian matrices for surface analysis.
  • Measure Theory: Long-term goal for a deeper understanding of probability.

docs.title: The Linear Approximation
docs.text: A derivative isn't just a slope; it's the best linear approximation of a function at a point. In 3DGS or Neural Rendering, we constantly linearize complex systems to make them computable.

docs.title: From 1D to \(\mathbb{R}^n\)
docs.text:
The jump from single-variable to multivariable analysis is where the magic happens.

  • The Gradient (\( abla f\)): Points in the direction of steepest ascent.
  • The Divergence (\( abla \cdot F\)): Measuring the "source" or "sink" of a vector field.
  • The Curl (\( abla imes F\)): Essential for understanding rotations in 3D space.

docs.title: 🛠️ Essential Formulae
docs.text: \[L(x) = f(a) + f'(a)(x - a)\] \[\nabla f(a) = \left( \frac{\partial f}{\partial x_1}(a), \dots, \frac{\partial f}{\partial x_n}(a) \right)\]

docs.title: 📖 Literature
docs.text:
• Principles of Mathematical Analysis (Rudin): The "Gold Standard" for rigor. Hard, but undeniably useful.
  • Calculus on Manifolds (Spivak): A modern approach to multivariable analysis using differential forms.

docs.title: 🎥 Visual Resources
docs.text:
• 3Blue1Brown - Essence of Calculus: For when the symbols get too abstract and I need to see the area under the curve.
  • ZJU Course Materials: Keeping my pace synchronized with the Turing Class curriculum.

"The purpose of analysis is to find the hidden simplicity in complex systems."

docs.title: Linear Algebra: The Geometry of Data
docs.text:
"The purely formal handle on algebra is a bit like a dry bone; the geometric intuition is the meat." — Adapted from 3Blue1Brown

Linear Algebra is the engine behind Computer Vision and Spatial Intelligence. Following the "Axler Way" (LADR), I focus on linear maps and operators rather than just pushing numbers around in matrices.

docs.title: 🎯 Current Focus (LADR Pathway)
docs.text:
• Vector Spaces & Subspaces: Understanding the "canvas" where linear transformations live.
  • Linear Maps: Treating matrices as representations of functions between vector spaces.
  • Eigenvalues & Eigenvectors: Finding the invariant directions of a transformation.
  • Inner Product Spaces: The foundation of geometry—orthogonality, projections, and the Adjoint operator.

docs.title: 🔍 3D Vision Applications
docs.text:
• Change of Basis: Crucial for transforming coordinates between Camera Space, World Space, and Screen Space.
  • Singular Value Decomposition (SVD): The "Swiss Army Knife" for solving Overdetermined Systems and Essential Matrix estimation.
  • Projective Geometry: Understanding how 3D points map to 2D planes via Homographies.

docs.title: 📝 Key Insights
docs.text:
• Determinants are Volume: A determinant isn't just a formula; it's the scaling factor of a transformation's volume.
  • Spectral Theorem: Why symmetric matrices (like Covariance matrices) are so well-behaved and useful for PCA.

docs.title: 🛠️ Working Sandbox
docs.text:
• Implement basic Matrix Decomposition in pure C (for the "Low-level" soul).
  • Visualize Span and Linear Independence using Manim/Python.
  • Master the Gram-Schmidt Process for orthonormalization.
